risk_domain.name: Token Approval Phishing
risk_domain.description: Phishing attacks exploiting ERC-20/ERC-721 token approval mechanisms to drain user assets

limitations: cannot forge signatures without private key
malicious contracts are eventually flagged by security databases

H1_unlimited_approval_risk.name: Unlimited Approval Risk
H1_unlimited_approval_risk.description: Approving an unlimited amount of tokens to any contract poses a high risk.

signals.name: unlimited_approval_amount
signals.description: Identifies calls to approve() or increaseAllowance() where the amount parameter is equal to or greater than the maximum value (2^255) for a given token standard.

signals.name: eip2612_unlimited_approval
signals.description: Detects calls to permit() or Permit2 where the amount parameter is set to its maximum value, indicating an unlimited approval.

signals.name: unverified_spender_contract
signals.description: Detects interactions with contracts that are not verified on a block explorer and were deployed within the last 24 hours.

recommendations.description: Ensure all approve() or increaseAllowance() calls are for finite token amounts.
Verify the spender contract on a reputable blockchain explorer before approving unlimited tokens.
Utilize EIP-2612 permit() for granting approvals, reducing risk of transaction fees and front-running.
Implement automated monitoring for approval calls exceeding a certain threshold or to unverified contracts.

H2_unverified_contract_spender.name: Unverified Contract Spender
H2_unverified_contract_spender.description: Using unverified smart contracts can result in unauthorized access or loss of funds.

signals.name: unverified_spender
signals.description: Identifies transactions where the spender is a smart contract that has not been verified on a block explorer.

signals.name: recently_deployed_spender
signals.description: Detects transactions where the spender is a smart contract that was deployed within the last 24 hours.

recommendations.description: Manually verify the smart contract before interacting.
Interact only with verified contracts from reputable block explorers.
Automatically verify the contract using a security audit service before spending funds.

H3_scam_address_detection.name: Scam Address Detection
H3_scam_address_detection.description: Interaction with known scam addresses indicates a high likelihood of fraud.

signals.name: unlimited_token_approval
signals.description: Check if the approve() or increaseAllowance() function is called with an amount equal to or greater than the maximum uint256 value (2^255). Also, check for EIP-2612 permit() calls with unlimited approval.

signals.name: unverified_spender_contract
signals.description: Identify if the spender contract is not verified on a block explorer and has been deployed within the last 24 hours.

signals.name: suspicious_transaction_volume
signals.description: Detect unusually high transaction volume or frequency from the same address within a short time frame.

signals.name: new_contract_interaction
signals.description: Look for interactions with contracts that are new and have not been tested or reviewed yet, indicating a higher risk of fraud.

recommendations.description: Block transactions from unverified contracts.
Manually verify the spender contract before approving tokens.
Set limits on token approvals to prevent unlimited allowances.
Set up alerts for suspicious transaction volumes.
Do not interact with new, untested contracts.

H4_suspicious_function_selector_u.name: Suspicious Function Selector Usage
H4_suspicious_function_selector_u.description: Execution of unknown function selectors could indicate malicious intent or unexpected behavior.

signals.name: unknown_function_selector
signals.description: Detection of function selectors that are not recognized or documented in the smart contract's source code.

signals.name: unusual_input_pattern
signals.description: Identify unusual input patterns to function selectors that deviate significantly from typical usage, such as unexpected parameters or frequency.

signals.name: external_call_to_unverified_contract
signals.description: External calls made to contracts whose source code has not been verified on a block explorer, indicating potential risk.

signals.name: recently_deployed_spender
signals.description: Detection of function selectors from spender contracts that were deployed within the last 24 hours, which may be untrusted or malicious.

recommendations.description: Verify the source code of unfamiliar smart contracts on a blockchain explorer.
Implement input validation and parameter checks to prevent unexpected behavior.
Use established, audited libraries instead of writing custom functions.
Set up alerts for unusual input patterns to suspicious function selectors.
Automatically verify new contracts and their functions using a script.

H5_stale_approval_risk.name: Stale Approval Risk
H5_stale_approval_risk.description: Unrevoked, unlimited token approvals pose a risk as they can be exploited over time.

signals.name: unlimited_approval
signals.description: Check if approve() or increaseAllowance() calls are made with an amount equal to the maximum value (2^256 - 1) or EIP-2612 Permit with unlimited approval.

signals.name: recent_unverified_spender
signals.description: Identify if the spender contract has been deployed recently and its code is not verified on a public blockchain explorer like Etherscan.

signals.name: stale_approval
signals.description: Detect if the token approval has been set to an amount greater than what is needed for the transaction, indicating potential misuse or accidental use of a high approval.

recommendations.description: Set approval limits to the minimum required amount for transactions.
Regularly monitor and verify newly deployed spender contracts on blockchain explorers.
Implement secure approval mechanisms like EIP-2612 for token allowances.
Automatically check and revoke unnecessary high approvals using smart contracts.

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring phishing detection rate

methodology.step_1: Collect dataset of known phishing transactions (Forta alerts, Scam Sniffer reports)
methodology.step_2: Collect dataset of legitimate DeFi approval transactions
methodology.step_4: Measure: true positive rate (phishing caught), false positive rate (legitimate flagged)

combined_benchmark.target: Catch >90% of known phishing patterns with <5% false positive rate

templates.summary: Scanned {n_txs} approval transactions. {n_risky} flagged ({pct_risky}%). Value at risk: ${value_at_risk}. Top risks: {top_risks}.
